---
title: "Team"
date-modified: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-note .draft}
**DISCLAIMER: This page is under construction**  
**Draft scaffold.** Replace placeholder bios with photos + 2-3 sentence
descriptions sourced from team CVs.
:::

## Investigators

### Laura B. Balzer — PI

Associate Professor of Biostatistics, University of California, Berkeley.
Developed Two-Stage TMLE and Adaptive Pre-specification; co-led the
SEARCH consortium analyses showing TMLE's gains over standard CRT
methods. 2014 Causality in Statistics Education Award.

### Mark van der Laan — Co-Investigator

Professor of Biostatistics, University of California, Berkeley. Inventor
of TMLE, Super Learner, and several multiple-testing procedures; author
of *Targeted Learning* (Springer, 2011).

### Joshua Schwab — Statistical programmer

Maintainer of the `TwoStageTMLE` R package.

## Scientific Advisory Board

* **Gabriel Chamie** — UCSF, Protocol Vice-Chair of SAPPHIRE
* **Jane Kabami** — Infectious Diseases Research Collaboration, Uganda; MPI of Peer-led DCP
* **James Ayieko** — Kenya Medical Research Institute

## External Review Board

Four internationally-recognized CRT methodologists provide ≥1×/year
feedback on dissemination materials:

* **Elizabeth Turner** — Duke University
* **Andrew Forbes** — Monash University
* **Karla Hemming** — University of Birmingham
* **Monica Taljaard** — University of Ottawa

## Contributing

Suggestions, corrections, and feedback are welcome — open an issue at
<https://github.com/LauraBalzer/TwoStageTMLE/issues>.
